pages.raw_text:
The workingmen lost out in the
fight for higher wages at the meet
ing of the committee of the whole
OF the city council. last night, but
they assert that the controversy
was only the first round in battle
which they are bound to win in
the end.


1t has resolved itself into a con-
troversy between the moneyed in
terests of the city against the labor
ing class said a prominent union
man this morning. THE chamber
of commerce has come out in the
open as the champion of the mon
eyed interests"


Councilman Schaefer showed by
actual facts and figures last night
that a workingman cannot live as
he should, and support his family
on $2.75 a day with the present
prices of necessities. He brought
the question to the problem of beef
steak, flour and clothing for the
workingman and his children, and
showed that the present wage is
too low.


H. L. Hughes said a few things
about the chamber of commerce.
